content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() method

fields.summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() method

content:
type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: My update() now calls a new helper self._update_key(key, value) for each key (iterable.keys()/pairs and kwds); _update_key does: if key not in self -> self[key]=value; elif current&value both dict -> current.update(value); self[key]=current; elif both Sequence with dict .feature -> current.feature.update(value.feature); self[key]=current; else self[key]=value. All paths end by going through __setitem__ so your decorator wrapping update() (or __setitem__) should compose fine without touching merge logic - just wrap update() as-is.
